summary: Small matching-focused fix in ftCo_Attack100.c for common fighter capture-damage-low routines. The PR rewrote ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys and fn_800DC624 through static inline helper bodies plus thin exported wrappers, adjusted local variable lifetimes/reloads, removed a dont_inline pragma block, and added PAD_STACK(4) to fn_800DC624. The decomp bot reported 3 new 100% matches in the ftCo_Attack100 unit and +672 matched bytes overall.

key_files.role: Only changed file; contains ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll, and fn_800DC624 matching fixes.

what_changed: Removed the local #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop wrapper around ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys and fn_800DC624.
Changed ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys from a direct function body into static inline ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline plus a public ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys wrapper that calls the inline helper.
Changed fn_800DC624 from a direct function body into static inline fn_800DC624_inline plus a public fn_800DC624 wrapper.
Added PAD_STACK(4) to the exported fn_800DC624 wrapper before calling fn_800DC624_inline.

Adjusted local variable usage in ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys: uses gobj directly instead of temp_r30, introduces Fighter* fp and Fighter* fp2, reloads GET_FIGHTER(gobj) before checking x2226_b2, and keeps a Vec3* pos for HSD_JObjSetTranslate.

Adjusted local variable usage in fn_800DC624: initializes Fighter* temp_r31 directly from GET_FIGHTER(gobj), uses gobj directly in calls, removes the temporary Vec3* pos, and passes &temp_r31->cur_pos directly to HSD_JObjSetTranslate.

The behavior-level flow remains the same in the diff: change to ftCo_MS_CaptureDamageHi, call fn_800DB5D8, call fn_800DAA40 with victim_gobj, optionally install fn_800DC384 via ft_80083C00 when x2226_b2 is false, then update the JObj translate from cur_pos.
smart_moves: Using static inline helper bodies preserved source readability while changing compiler inlining/codegen at the exported function boundary.
The thin wrapper pattern let fn_800DC624 carry an explicit PAD_STACK(4) without cluttering the logical helper body.
Reloading GET_FIGHTER(gobj) into fp2 before the x2226_b2 check in ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys appears to have been an intentional codegen/lifetime control point rather than a semantic change.
Removing the dont_inline pragma and replacing it with targeted inline helpers gave finer control over which code was emitted inline and which symbol remained externally callable.
The patch was tightly scoped to one ftCommon source file and one cluster of related capture-damage-low functions, making the matching impact easy to verify.
decomp_lessons: When a function nearly matches but stack/register behavior is off, try splitting the implementation into a static inline helper and a thin public wrapper instead of relying only on dont_inline pragmas.
PAD_STACK in a wrapper can be a useful way to match expected stack allocation while leaving the decompiled logical body clean.
GET_FIGHTER reload placement can matter for matching; preserving or forcing a second load may be necessary around state-changing calls.
Direct use of the original argument gobj versus assigning it to temp_r30 can affect register allocation and should be tested rather than assumed equivalent.
Small local expression changes, such as keeping a Vec3* temporary in one function but passing &temp_r31->cur_pos directly in another, can be decisive for matching similar routines.

Bot match reports are useful evidence for validating tactical codegen-only rewrites: this PR moved fn_800DC624, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, and ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll to 100% according to the GALE01 report.
naming_conventions: Static inline helper functions used the original exported function name with an _inline suffix: ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline and fn_800DC624_inline.
The rewritten ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys uses conventional Fighter pointer names fp and fp2 rather than register-style temp_r30/temp_r31 for most locals.
fn_800DC624_inline retained the register-derived name temp_r31 for its Fighter* local, suggesting register-style temporary names may still be kept when they help document or preserve a match.
Existing project macros and typedef names were preserved: Fighter_GObj, HSD_GObj, GET_FIGHTER, GET_JOBJ, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, HSD_JObjSetTranslate.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace broad #pragma dont_inline usage with static inline helper plus exported wrapper to tune inlining and emitted symbol boundaries.
Use PAD_STACK(4) in a wrapper when the target function needs a specific stack shape.
Control pointer live ranges by introducing or removing temporary variables: fp, fp2, temp_r31, pos.
Force a GET_FIGHTER reload before a field test when the target assembly likely reloads the fighter pointer after intervening calls.
Use direct argument gobj in call sites instead of a copied Fighter_GObj* temp when that better matches register allocation.
Choose between a Vec3* temporary and an inline address expression based on the target codegen; this PR uses both forms in different helper bodies.

review_feedback.text: GALE01 report showed matched code 55.46% (+0.02%, +672 bytes) and 3 new matches.
No human review comments or PR body rationale were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other ftCommon capture routines where static inline helper plus wrapper could replace dont_inline pragmas for matching.
Search for PAD_STACK wrappers near ftCo_Attack100 or capture-damage functions to compare stack-matching patterns.
Search for uses of GET_FIGHTER reloads before x2226_b2 checks to identify similar codegen-sensitive spots.
Inspect nearby functions using fn_800DC384, fn_800DB5D8, and fn_800DAA40 for repeated CaptureDamageHi transition patterns.
Check whether ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll reached 100% only as a side effect of neighboring code layout or due to changes in this hunk.
